name: Greg's Favourite Pasta
recipeInstructions:
1. Mix dry ingredients on a board, forming a well in the center.
2. Add olive oil and eggs to the well. Mix with a fork by gradually blending the dry ingredients into the egg mixture. When it becomes too stiff to use a fork, begin kneading by hand until the dough reaches a very stiff consistency. If necessary, add warm water in small increments.
3. Allow the dough to rest while covered for at least 10 minutes.
4. Using a floured board, cut off pieces of dough about the size of an egg.
5. Flatten each piece with the palm of your hand.
6. Process through the pasta machine starting on the thickest setting.
7. Roll through the machine again at each progressively thinner setting to form long strips the width of your machine and the proper thickness for the desired style of pasta (this helps incorporate the ingredients).
8. Gently rub a small amount of flour on the surface and allow it to air dry for about 30 minutes.
9. Attach the cutting head for the desired pasta type.
10. Cut, lay out on a floured board, sprinkle lightly with flour, and allow to dry further before cooking or rolling in freezer paper and freezing for later.
Remember, fresh pasta cooks much faster than the cardboard box variety. Don't over-cook. If cut with a medium width head, these make great chicken and noodles or cut thin for spaghetti. By using 1 egg per cup of flour, you get a slightly golden pasta that looks and tastes great. For those more health-conscious, reduce the number of eggs and replace them with water. A little saffron will restore the rich color.
